[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2022-03-31
[TITLE]: Images From 2017 Peddled As Recent To Show Reactions To Oscar Slapgate
[METADATA/INTRO]: Social media posts claim a collage of Hollywood celebrities' stunned faces shows their reactions to Will Smith hitting Chris Rock on stage during the 2022 Academy Awards ceremony. This is false; the images are from 2017, when "La La Land"...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: From left to right, top to bottom, the photo collage shows: Matt Damon, Emma Stone, Trevante Rhodes, David Oyelowo, Charlize Theron, Dwayne Johnson, Busy Philipps, Barry Jenkins, Meryl Streep, Jaden Piner, Salma Hayek, John Legend, Michelle Williams, Mel Gibson and Ryan Gosling. 
 AFP Fact Check found that the still images of the audience were taken during the Best Picture mix-up at the 2017 Academy Awards ceremony. 
 The full clip of the incident -- which includes singer and actor John Legend's reaction -- is available on the Oscars YouTube channel here. 
 Actor Trevante Rhodes and director Barry Jenkins, both of "Moonlight," can be seen reacting to the news their movie won Best Picture here.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2022-03-29
[TITLE]: Fact Check: Does this viral collage show celebs reacting to Will Smith-Chris Rock slap incident? Here is the truth | Trending
[METADATA/INTRO]: Fact Check: Does this viral collage show celebs reacting to Will Smith-Chris Rock slap incident? Here is the truth No, this viral pic doesn’t show celebs reacting to Will Smith-Chris Rock slap incident that took place in Oscars 2022....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Claim: “Audience reaction when Will Smith punched Chris Rock on Stage at the #Oscars,” wrote a Twitter user while posting the picture. The collage comprises images of Matt Damon, Emma Stone, Trevante Rhodes, David Oyelowo, Charlize Theron, Dwayne Johnson, Busy Philipps, Barry Jenkins, Meryl Streep, Jaden Piner, Salma Hayek, John Legend, Michelle Williams, Mel Gibson and Ryan Gosling. 
 Take a look at the picture that are now many sharing: 
 Investigation: A Google search using the keywords ‘celebrity reaction collage Oscars’ presented us with an image that was tweeted by Los Angeles Times back in 2017. The image shows the reactions of celebrities to a mixup that happened when La La Land was mistakenly announced as the winner instead of Moonlight at Oscars 2017.

[TITLE]: This photo sums up the end of the 89th Academy Awards pretty well
[METADATA/INTRO]: This photo sums up the end of the 89th Academy Awards pretty well "No, there's a mistake. 'Moonlight,' you guys won Best Picture."...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: This photo sums up the end of the 89th Academy Awards pretty well 
 "No, there's a mistake. 'Moonlight,' you guys won Best Picture." 
 "No, there's a mistake. 'Moonlight,' you guys won Best Picture." 
 This image really sums up how fans felt at the end of the 89th annual Academy Awards. 
 The looks of shock and confusion on the faces of celebrities in the crowd mirrored the looks on the faces of many watching at home. The event was both the first and third most talked about moments on Twitter Sunday night. 
 This photo runs the gambit of emotions felt during the mix-up, which has already been nick-named envelopegate.
